title: sarothamnus striatus var. procerus
intro: Sarothamnus striatus var. procerus, also known as the procumbent broom or procumbent broom-rape, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the circulatory system and promote sweating, which has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant contains various bioactive compounds that have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating congestion. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, to treat various ailments, and it can also be found in some modern herbal supplements.
